_comment: Copy this file to data/YYYY-MM-DD.json, fill in today's values, then run: python3 scripts/publish_brief.py data/YYYY-MM-DD.json

storage.callout_html: The +59 Bcf print came in 21 Bcf bearish versus the 5-yr average implied build — surplus versus the 5-yr average is actively widening each report week.
storage.narrative_html: You are on the steepest start-of-season trajectory toward 4,000+ Bcf by November. EIA's April STEO projects end-October inventory at 4,015 Bcf, 6% above 5-yr average, on the back of production growth and constrained export capacity. That's a structural price ceiling on any summer rally unless demand surprises materially to the upside.

production.record_note_html: January 2026 dry gas production was the highest for any January since 1973.
production.regional_html: Northeast output has slipped roughly 1 Bcf/d from earlier-year highs to ~35 Bcf/d — typical shoulder-season softness ahead of the summer ramp.
production.waha_html: Waha remains structurally broken: Permian production and pipeline takeaway constraints have kept Waha prices negative for 82% of 2026 trading days. Associated gas from continued Permian oil drilling is the key incremental supply vector — it's price-insensitive to Henry Hub, which is why the supply response to $2.60 prompt is muted.

weather.season_html: The 2025–26 winter heating season ranked as the second-warmest in the Lower 48 since 2019–20, with utility-weighted HDDs 6.5% warmer than the 30-year normal.
weather.forward_html: Forward look: DTN is calling for the mildest summer cooling season since 2017 as El Niño emerges by May. Combined with elevated pre-summer inventories, new Permian associated gas takeaway coming online in 3Q26, and decelerating LNG growth, the setup skews risks to the downside later this year.

risk_flags.title: Storage surplus is widening, not stabilizing.
risk_flags.body_html: +108 Bcf vs. 5-yr avg with mildest summer forecast since 2017 = high probability the surplus widens through Q3. Any position that requires a price catalyst above $3.00 prompt faces headwinds.

risk_flags.title: Waha is uninvestable on directional length.
risk_flags.body_html: 82% negative-print rate YTD — this is a takeaway story, not a demand story.

risk_flags.title: Northeast shoulder softness is a nomination risk.
risk_flags.body_html: At ~35 Bcf/d with mild weather, you're in the zone where producers may curtail proactively or pipelines issue OFOs on pack constraints if a cold shot comes through unexpectedly — watch Algonquin and Tennessee 200L nomination cycles.

risk_flags.title: LNG is the swing variable that offsets bearish storage math.
risk_flags.body_html: U.S. LNG exports were estimated at 17.9 Bcf/d in March, the second-highest monthly volume on record. Any Hormuz de-escalation that narrows the HH-TTF spread could pull feedgas volumes and flip the storage trajectory.

actions: Check Apr 23 storage print consensus now — if the market is pricing +65–70 Bcf vs. a print likely in the +60–70 range, there's no edge in the number itself; trade the miss vs. weather revisions.
Pull Waha basis curve on NYMEX — if basis is less negative than spot realized YTD, there may be a short-basis trade into 3Q26 takeaway additions.
Map LNG feedgas daily flows (Sabine, Plaquemines, Corpus Christi) — 20 Bcf/d is the demand anchor. Any slippage below 18 Bcf/d is a storage bull-case killer.
Watch NOAA 8-14 day for any early CDDs in Texas/Southeast that would tighten the injection trajectory.
primers: Primer on Storage Mechanics (injection season math, surplus/deficit framework); Primer on Pipeline Mechanics (Waha takeaway, Algonquin constraints)
sim: T2, Waha/Permian, shoulder-season takeaway constraint with positive storage delta — run natgas-trader-ops-sim with those inputs to model the basis trade setup.
index_summary: Storage at 1,970 Bcf (+108 Bcf vs. 5-yr avg, surplus widening). Production ~109.3 Bcf/d. Mildest summer forecast since 2017. Four VP risk flags: storage surplus trajectory, Waha uninvestable on length, NE nomination risk, LNG as swing variable.
